MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication On All Claims
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elizabeth Pederson, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3862, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication On All Claims. The motion is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the wrongful death cause of action. Plaintiff fails to highlight any facts creating a duty of care under the traditional Rowland v. Christian, (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108, 113, factors or based on unusual police conduct that created a special relationship between Mr. Yount and defendant. See Adams v. City of Fremont (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 243. Defendant did not encourage Mr. Yount to jump. Defendant did not owe a duty to arrest the hecklers; defendant was focused on Mr. Yount and securing the scene. While Officer Perez called Mr. Yount a fool, he told him to go back inside. Plaintiff speculates that Mr. Yount heard defendant and the hecklers that encouraged Mr. Yount to jump. Lugtu v. California Highway Patrol (2001) 26 Cal.4th 703, is distinguishable. In that action, the police officer increased the risk of danger to the injured party. Plaintiff cannot establish the Bane Act cause of action and cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Those claims were not part of plaintiff's government claim. See Nelson v. County of Los Angeles (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 783, 796. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Set For Trial On Jul-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
